new_beliefs.claim: Build the belief-card renderer: a mix task rendering any belief as a readable markdown mirror (claim, grounding, evidence, deps as resolved links) with a write-time locator back to its JSON definition - the middle tier of the in-document reference convention (gloss, card, substrate). Cards are derived views, regenerated on demand, never authoritative; the JSON is the graph.

evidence.detail: From the glue-UI exploration: cb references in conversation surfaces are positive alignment signals the operator cannot parse raw; the card is the readability layer between the gloss and the substrate. Prototyped as a scratch script rendering tmp/cards/ during the session; this directive is the productization. Preflight 2026-06-11: backlog/observability tag-overlap with sibling backlog directives only; no semantic conflict.

new_beliefs.claim: Settle the in-document belief-reference convention for glue surfaces: every cb reference carries an italic gloss (enough meaning to keep reading), a link to its rendered card, and a link to the JSON definition at a write-time-resolved line. Settle it through use in the transcript/lap-log surfaces, then encode the settled form as a convention directive depping on a497.

evidence.detail: Three-tier form demonstrated live in tmp/transcript.md during the exploration's first session; deliberately left as an action-item rather than a convention - the form should survive a few weeks of real reading before it hardens into canon. Preflight 2026-06-11: backlog/observability tag-overlap with sibling backlog directives only; no semantic conflict.
